# Cluster reconstruction requests

Files in this directory are generated by the `tofu-reconstruct.yml`
workflow and consumed by `cluster-reset.yml` / `cluster-reinstall.yml`
plus tofu's per-node trigger logic.

## File naming

```
<operation>-<utc-timestamp>-<run-id>.yaml
```

- `<operation>` is `reset` or `reinstall`
- `<utc-timestamp>` is `YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ`
- `<run-id>` is the GitHub Actions run id of the workflow that opened the request PR

The prefix is what each downstream workflow's path filter matches:
`reset-*.yaml` triggers `cluster-reset.yml`, `reinstall-*.yaml` triggers
`cluster-reinstall.yml`.

## File schema

```yaml
operation: reset | reinstall
scope: all | selected
nodes: ["<node_key>", ...]   # required when scope=selected, ignored otherwise
reason: |-
  Free-text reason. Shown in the PR title and body.
requested_by: <github-actor>
requested_at: <iso-8601-timestamp>
source_run_id: "<github-run-id>"
source_run_url: <https://github.com/.../actions/runs/...>
```

## How tofu consumes these

`tofu/layers/01-contabo-infra/reconstruction.tf` and
`tofu/layers/02-oracle-infra/reconstruction.tf` glob `reinstall-*.yaml`
in this directory and compute, per node:

- `per_node_reinstall_request_hash[node_key]` — SHA1 of the latest
  request file that mentions this node (via `scope=all` or `nodes`
  containing the key), or `""` if none. This drives the per-node
  trigger that recreates `null_resource.ensure_image` (Contabo) or
  replaces `terraform_data.reinstall_marker` (OCI), in turn driving
  the disk wipe.

- `cluster_wide_reinstall_marker` (Contabo only) — SHA1 of all
  `scope=all` request hashes. This flows through layer 03 and triggers
  re-bootstrap when every CP was wiped together.

## Pruning

Files here are append-only by design — the audit trail of when and
why the cluster was reset/reinstalled. Old files can be safely
deleted once their per-node hash is no longer the latest applicable
one for any in-cluster node (i.e. a newer request has superseded
them, or the listed nodes are no longer in inventory). Removing an
older file does not change any per-node hash, so no downstream
trigger drift.
